title: TENSURE: Fuzzing Sparse Tensor Compilers (Registered Report)
authors: Kabilan Mahathevan, Yining Zhang, Muhammad Ali Gulzar, Kirshanthan Sundararajah
abstract: Sparse Tensor Compilers (STCs) have emerged as critical infrastructure for optimizing high-dimensional data analytics and machine learning workloads. The STCs must synthesize complex, irregular control flow for various compressed storage formats directly from high-level declarative specifications, thereby making them highly susceptible to subtle correctness defects. Existing testing frameworks, which rely on mutating computation graphs restricted to a standard vocabulary of operators, fail to exercise the arbitrary loop synthesis capabilities of these compilers. Furthermore, generic grammar-based fuzzers struggle to generate valid inputs due to the strict rules governing how indices are reused across multiple tensors. In this paper, we present TENSURE, the first extensible black-box fuzzing framework specifically designed for the testing of STCs. TENSURE leverages Einstein Summation (Einsum) notation as a general input abstraction, enabling the generation of complex, unconventional tensor contractions that expose corner cases in the code-generation phases of STCs. We propose a novel constraint-based generation algorithm that guarantees 100% semantic validity of synthesized kernels, significantly outperforming the ~3.3% validity rate of baseline grammar fuzzers. To enable metamorphic testing without a trusted reference, we introduce a set of semantic-preserving mutation operators that exploit algebraic commutativity and heterogeneity in storage formats. Our evaluation on two state-of-the-art systems, TACO and Finch, reveals widespread fragility, particularly in TACO, where TENSURE exposed crashes or silent miscompilations in a majority of generated test cases. These findings underscore the critical need for specialized testing tools in the sparse compilation ecosystem.
title_zh: TENSURE：针对稀疏张量编译器的模糊测试（注册报告）
abstract_zh:
稀疏张量编译器（Sparse Tensor Compilers, STCs）已成为优化高维数据分析和机器学习工作负载的关键基础设施。STCs 必须从高级声明式规范中直接综合出针对各种压缩存储格式的复杂且不规则的控制流，这使得它们极易出现细微的正确性缺陷。现有的测试框架依赖于对运算图进行变异，但这些运算图仅限于标准算子词汇表，因此无法有效检验编译器在任意循环综合方面的能力。此外，通用的基于语法的模糊测试工具由于受到多个张量间索引重用规则的严格限制，难以生成有效的输入。

本文提出了 TENSURE，这是首个可扩展的黑盒模糊测试框架，专为测试稀疏张量编译器而设计。TENSURE 采用爱因斯坦求和（Einsum）符号作为通用输入抽象，能够生成复杂且非传统的张量收缩操作，从而揭示 STCs 在代码生成阶段的边界情况。我们提出了一种新颖的基于约束的生成算法，确保所合成内核的语义有效性达到 100%，远超基线语法模糊器约 3.3% 的有效率。为了在缺乏可信参考实现的情况下支持变质测试（metamorphic testing），我们引入了一组保持语义不变的变异操作符，利用代数交换性以及存储格式的异构性来生成等价但结构不同的测试用例。

我们在两个最先进的系统——TACO 和 Finch 上进行了评估，结果揭示了这些系统普遍存在脆弱性，尤其是在 TACO 中，TENSURE 在大多数生成的测试用例中均触发了崩溃或静默的错误编译。这些发现凸显了在稀疏编译生态系统中开发专用测试工具的迫切需求。

title: Controller Datapath Aware Verification of Masked Hardware Generated via High Level Synthesis
authors: Nilotpola Sarma, Vaishali Ghanshyam Chaudhuri, Chandan Karfa
abstract: Masking is a countermeasure against Power Side Channel Attacks (PSCAs) in both software and hardware implementations of cryptographic algorithms. Compared to software masking, implementing masked hardware is time consuming and error prone. Recent approaches, therefore, rely on High Level Synthesis (HLS) tools to automatically generate masked Register Transfer Level (RTL) hardware from verified masked software, significantly reducing design effort. Since HLS was never developed for security, HLS optimizations may impact PSCA security of the generated RTL. As a result, verifying the PSCA security of HLS generated masked RTL is crucial. Existing hardware masking verification tools can verify masked hardware, but may produce false positives when applied to HLS generated designs with controller datapath architectures obtained due to resource-shared datapath obtained via HLS. This work proposes a hardware masking verification strategy for HLS generated masked hardware. Our toolflow MaskedHLSVerif, performs state-wise formal verification of controller datapath RTL obtained via HLS, thereby avoiding false positives caused by resource-shared datapaths. Our tool flow correctly verifies standard cryptographic benchmarks, consisting of cascaded masked gadgets and the PRESENT S-box masked with gadgets, where existing tools like REBECCA reports false positives. The proposed tool-flow is able to detect masking flaws induced by HLS-optimizations as well.
title_zh: 基于高电平综合生成的掩码硬件的控制器数据路径感知验证
abstract_zh:
掩码技术是抵御密码算法软硬件实现中功耗侧信道攻击（PSCA）的一种有效对策。与软件掩码相比，硬件掩码的实现过程耗时且容易出错。因此，近期的研究方法依赖于高级综合（HLS）工具，从经过验证的掩码软件自动生成掩码的寄存器传输级（RTL）硬件设计，从而显著降低设计开销。然而，由于HLS工具最初并非为安全目的而设计，其优化过程可能会影响生成RTL设计的PSCA安全性。因此，对HLS生成的掩码RTL设计进行PSCA安全性验证至关重要。

现有的硬件掩码验证工具虽可验证掩码硬件，但在应用于因HLS资源共享数据路径而产生的控制器-数据路径架构设计时，可能会产生误报（false positives）。本文提出了一种针对HLS生成的掩码硬件的掩码验证策略。我们开发的工具流MaskedHLSVerif，能够对通过HLS获得的控制器-数据路径RTL设计进行逐状态的形式化验证，从而避免由资源共享数据路径引起的误报问题。在标准密码学基准测试（包括级联掩码模块以及使用模块实现掩码的PRESENT S盒）上，本工具流能够正确完成验证，而现有工具（如REBECCA）在这些案例中会产生误报。此外，所提出的工具流还能够检测由HLS优化引入的掩码漏洞。

title: Sudoku: Refining DRAM Address Mapping through Advanced Timing and Configuration Analysis
authors: Minbok Wi, Wanju Doh, Seungmin Baek, Seonyong Park, Mattan Erez, Jung Ho Ahn
abstract: Understanding undocumented DRAM address-mapping functions is crucial for enabling well-known attacks, such as RowHammer, and for gaining deeper insights into memory system behavior. Prior software-based reverse-engineering approaches primarily rely on access latency differences caused by row-buffer conflicts to identify functions that distinguish DRAM banks. Other works aim to decompose DRAM address mappings into component-level details, either operating only on specific CPU platforms or being limited to identifying functions up to the rank level, without revealing finer-grained DRAM components. In this paper, we propose Sudoku, a software-only tool that automatically decomposes full DRAM address mappings into channel, rank, bank group, and bank functions. Sudoku first distinguishes channel-indexing functions by exploiting bandwidth differences across DRAM address-mapping functions. It then leverages two newly identified timing channels to decompose the remaining address-mapping functions: (1) refresh-interval-induced latency variations and (2) timing constraints arising from consecutive memory accesses. Together, these techniques enable Sudoku to refine DRAM address mappings into component-level functions, allowing it to identify not only banks but also finer-grained DRAM components that were previously difficult to distinguish. We evaluate Sudoku on recent Intel and AMD processors and demonstrate that it successfully decomposes DRAM address mappings into component-level functions.
title_zh: Sudoku：通过高级时序和配置分析优化DRAM地址映射
abstract_zh: 理解未公开的DRAM地址映射函数对于实现诸如RowHammer等知名攻击以及深入洞察内存系统行为至关重要。以往基于软件的逆向工程方法主要依赖由行缓冲区冲突引起的访问延迟差异，来识别区分DRAM存储体（bank）的函数。其他研究工作则试图将DRAM地址映射分解为更细粒度的组件级细节，但这些方法要么仅适用于特定CPU平台，要么只能识别到“rank”（列）级别，无法揭示更细粒度的DRAM组件。本文提出Sudoku，一种纯软件工具，可自动将完整的DRAM地址映射分解为通道（channel）、列（rank）、存储体组（bank group）和存储体（bank）的映射函数。Sudoku首先通过利用不同DRAM地址映射函数导致的带宽差异，区分出通道索引函数；随后，借助两种新发现的时序信道，进一步分解剩余的地址映射函数：（1）由刷新间隔引起的延迟变化；（2）连续内存访问带来的时序约束。这些技术共同使Sudoku能够将DRAM地址映射细化为组件级函数，不仅能识别存储体，还能识别以往难以区分的更细粒度DRAM组件。我们在最新的Intel和AMD处理器上对Sudoku进行了评估，结果表明其能够成功将DRAM地址映射分解为组件级函数。

title: Feature Extraction of UAV and Bird via ISAC Base Station: From Algorithm to Hardware Verification
authors: Jiachen Wei, Dingyou Ma, Zongqi Mo, Zhiqing Wei, Ningyan Guo, Kan Yu, Qixun Zhang
abstract: With the rapid development of the low-altitude economy, the widespread deployment of unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs) necessitates effective sensing technologies for airspace monitoring. Integrated sensing and communication (ISAC) enables mobile communication base stations (BSs) to function as sensing nodes, providing a promising solution for UAV detection. Accurate identification of UAVs often relies on the extraction of distinctive micro-Doppler signatures generated by their rotating blades. However, in urban environments, these signatures are frequently obscured due to low signal-to-noise ratio (SNR) and strong dynamic interference from vehicles, pedestrians, and, in particular, birds. To address this challenge, this paper proposes a robust micro-Doppler feature extraction method based on multicarrier integration and a rotor micro-Doppler null space pursuit (rmD-NSP) algorithm. In one real-world scenario, both a bird and a UAV appeared within the same range cell, with the UAV’s micro-Doppler signals heavily masked by the bird’s strong reflections. After applying the proposed algorithm, distinct micro-Doppler features are successfully extracted, revealing approximately eight rotor blade flashes of the UAV within a 0.1s interval and two wingbeat cycles of the bird within the 0.5s observation window.
title_zh: 通过ISAC基站进行无人机与鸟类的特征提取：从算法到硬件验证
abstract_zh: 随着低空经济的快速发展，无人机（UAV）的大规模部署对空域监测提出了有效的感知技术需求。集成感知与通信（ISAC）技术使得移动通信基站（BS）可同时作为感知节点，为无人机检测提供了有前景的解决方案。准确识别无人机通常依赖于提取其旋转桨叶产生的独特微多普勒特征。然而，在城市环境中，由于信噪比（SNR）较低，以及车辆、行人尤其是鸟类带来的强烈动态干扰，这些特征常常被掩盖。为应对这一挑战，本文提出了一种基于多载波融合和转子微多普勒零空间追踪（rmD-NSP）算法的鲁棒微多普勒特征提取方法。在一个真实场景中，一只鸟和一架无人机同时出现在同一距离单元内，无人机的微多普勒信号被鸟类强烈的回波严重遮蔽。应用所提算法后，成功提取出清晰的微多普勒特征，在0.1秒的时间间隔内识别出无人机约八次旋翼闪烁，在0.5秒的观测窗口内识别出鸟类两次完整的翅膀拍打周期。

title: Verilog-Driven Simulation and RTL Architecture of the SPECK Cipher for High-Performance Cryptography in VLSI Systems
publish: 2025 3rd International Conference on Advances in Computation, Communication and Information Technology (ICAICCIT)
abstract: In order to ensure data security in resources like wireless sensor networks, built-in systems, and Internet of Things devices, cryptography is essential, which is the background. The NSA created the Speck algorithm in the early part of 2000's as a lightweight encryption block that strikes a compromise between security and effectiveness. The design, simulation, and Verilog implementation of the RTL algorithm are the main objectives of this project. This work's main motivation & objective are creating an efficient Verilog code and using simulation to confirm its accuracy. In order to optimize area, performance, and decryption, the design incorporates key expansion, encryption, and decryption procedures. Testing shots are used to mimic and confirm the validity of the verilog-based design. Through the effective and scalable implementation of Speck-based verilog, appropriate for IoT and built-in systems, this study advances current hardware security research. Expressions for indexes Verilog, design RTL, encryption, key spot, expansion, simulation, light cryptography, decryption are used for problem statement.
title_zh: 基于Verilog的SPECK密码在VLSI系统中用于高性能密码学的仿真与RTL架构
abstract_zh: 为了确保无线传感器网络、嵌入式系统和物联网设备等资源中的数据安全，密码学至关重要，这是本研究的背景。美国国家安全局（NSA）在21世纪初设计了Speck算法，作为一种轻量级分组加密算法，旨在安全性和效率之间取得平衡。本项目的主要目标是完成该算法的RTL设计、仿真及Verilog实现。本工作的核心动机与目标是编写高效的Verilog代码，并通过仿真验证其正确性。该设计集成了密钥扩展、加密和解密过程，以优化面积、性能和解密效率。通过测试激励对基于Verilog的设计进行模拟，以验证其有效性。本研究通过实现高效且可扩展的基于Speck算法的Verilog设计，适用于物联网和嵌入式系统，从而推动了当前硬件安全领域的研究进展。本文问题陈述中使用的关键术语包括：Verilog、RTL设计、加密、密钥生成、密钥扩展、仿真、轻量级密码学、解密。
